methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
POLARIS Raumflugzeuge is a Series A–stage space and aerospace company founded by Alexander Kopp. The valuation universe comprises 13 comparable firms: 5 public companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, Planet Labs, Impulse Space, and Apex Space.

Two standard triangulation methods could not be applied. Peer-Median analysis requires a minimum of three same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables; the Space & Aerospace sector yielded only 2 adjacent-stage peers and 0 same-stage matches from the 11 total sector firms, falling short of the threshold. Public-Comp Anchoring using the sector's median P/S multiple of 25.8x across the 6 public tickers is inapplicable because POLARIS's revenue is not on file—standard practice for private companies.

Without quantitative inputs from either method, a specific valuation range cannot be supported by this data. The analysis identifies structural constraints: the target operates in a well-capitalized but fragmented sector with meaningful public market comparables (median P/S 25.8x) but insufficient private peer clustering at the Series A stage. Key uncertainties include POLARIS's current revenue run rate, unit economics within reusable aerospace platforms, and competitive positioning relative to the identified peer set. A credible valuation would require either disclosure of revenue metrics or a shift toward alternative methodologies (DCF, precedent transactions) grounded in company-specific data.
